title: Regmus alpinus
taxonomy: Regmus alpinus is a plant species within the family Berberidaceae. It is commonly known as the alpine rhododendron or alpine rose. Regmus alpinus is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of the western Himalayas. The plant is classified as a perennial species, which means it regenerates new growth each year from the same roots. As of my knowledge cutoff in 2023, Regmus alpinus is further classified within the genus Regmus, although it is sometimes placed within the genus Rhododendron.

intro: Regmus alpinus, also known as Rhodiola rosea, is a perennial flowering plant that grows in cold and high-altitude regions. The health benefits of Rhodiola rosea include reducing stress and anxiety, improving mental performance and mood, and enhancing physical endurance, making it a popular adaptogen in herbal medicine. The bioactive constituents of Rhodiola rosea include rosmarinic acid, rosavin, and salidroside, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Rhodiola rosea is commonly prepared as a tincture, capsule, or tea, and is often used to support mental health, reduce fatigue, and improve overall well-being.
